what_it_does: Declares a planned surface specification for view D3, titled 'Act-on-output menu', in area D, anchored to journey J1, and lists the UI addresses and build targets it must realize.
observations: It is a generated surface-spec, not a hand-authored design document
Home journey is J1 and journeys contains only J1; cross_journeys is empty
Represents three facets: INB-surface, ENG-force, RHM-verbs
Targets nine ui:// addresses across five components: canvas, chat, inspector, scenario-player, tabbar
Build instructions include a design lint command and a token-to-CSS pipeline
standouts: The surface is explicitly tied to a single journey spine (J1)
It maps abstract forces/verbs onto concrete UI addresses
Build_to couples components, a lint gate, and a token source
conventions: Uses a structured ledger schema with id, area, title, platforms, represents, and status
Lists concrete ui:// addresses rather than inventing them
References external build tooling and token files by path or command
Symbols array is empty because this is a design substrate, not a code file
concerns: Status 'planned' means the spec is not yet realized
No cross_journeys even though the surface spans multiple components, which may need cross-journey links later
The design_lint command is given as a shell snippet without version or environment context
notes: This is the design ledger entry for the 'Act-on-output menu' surface, specifying which UI addresses and build artifacts it must cover under journey J1.
questions: What do INB-surface, ENG-force, and RHM-verbs specifically denote?
How are the nine ui:// addresses validated against the listed components?
What is the relationship between D3 and other surfaces in the J1 directory?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be a generated surface-spec for view D3; the file does exactly that by declaring identity, scope, addresses, and build targets.
intention_signals: It is trying to pin a concrete UI surface (an act-on-output menu) to a stable set of addresses and build targets so that design and engineering can coordinate around one planned view.
novelty: It treats 'INB-surface', 'ENG-force', and 'RHM-verbs' as first-class representational tags alongside conventional platform and component metadata.
contribution: It contributes the scoped definition of view D3: its title, journey anchor, represented forces/verbs, the exact UI addresses it must satisfy, and the build pipeline (components, lint, tokens) needed to realize it.
summary_for_embedding: A planned surface specification for view D3 ('Act-on-output menu') in area D, anchored to journey J1, representing INB-surface, ENG-force, and RHM-verbs, and mapping to nine ui:// addresses across canvas, chat, inspector, scenario-player, and tabbar with build-time lint and token sourcing.
intention_for_embedding: This file was generated to record the design scope and build targets for the 'Act-on-output menu' surface so it can be implemented consistently across desktop and mobile.
